---
name: Feature request
about: Suggest an improvement that fits the W.A.Y? scope
labels: enhancement
---

<!--
W.A.Y? has a deliberately narrow scope. Before writing, it helps to picture where
your idea lands. The harness exists to serve one person well, around three emphases:
  (1) for non-developers — no code required to use it;
  (2) reliability — anti-hallucination (SVOP), honest reporting, source verification;
  (3) structure — a small set of load-bearing rules that work the same everywhere.
Ideas that strengthen one of these tend to fit. Ideas that turn W.A.Y? into a
general-purpose multi-user framework usually do not — and that's okay, it's a
boundary, not a judgment.
-->

## Proposal

A clear, plain-language description of what you'd like W.A.Y? to do.

## Motivation

What problem does this solve for you? What does the harness make you re-explain,
re-do, or guess at today that this would fix?

## Where it fits

Which of the three emphases does this serve, and roughly where would it live?

- [ ] Non-developer experience (onboarding, review, plain-language flow)
- [ ] Reliability (SVOP / honesty modes / source fetch / Best-of-N)
- [ ] Structure (rules, principles, full-loop, memory tiers)
- [ ] Other (please explain — and note if it might fall outside W.A.Y?'s scope)

## Alternatives considered

Other approaches you thought about, and why they fall short — including doing
nothing, or solving it outside the harness.

## Additional context

Anything else: examples, links, or sketches.

> **Please keep personal information and real operational data out of this issue**
> (real names, emails, private numbers, customer data). The harness isolates
> operational data by design (principle P6); issues should follow the same rule.
